file_name: GentechHoldingsInc_20190808_1-A_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_11776814_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_Distributor Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: Company desires to appoint Distributor as its exclusive distributor of the Products to all Clients that Distributor introduce, and upon confirmed the first order, this client come to be exclusive of Distributor, every new customer introduced by Distributor, must be approved by the company prior to any sale. After official approval from the Company, the customer introduced by Distributor, will appear on an official list in this contract, and will only be met by the Distributor and will follow the rules of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Company desires to appoint Distributor as a non-exclusive distributor of the Products to all Clients that Distributor introduce, and upon confirmed the first order, this client will not be exclusive of Distributor, every new customer introduced by Distributor, does not need to be approved by the company prior to any sale. Customers introduced by Distributor, may not appear on an official list in this contract, and may be met by parties other than the Distributor and may not follow the rules of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'exclusive distributor' to 'non-exclusive distributor' and removing the need for company approval introduces a direct contradiction with Section 1(a) regarding exclusivity. It creates an in-text contradiction about the nature of the distributorship itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: APPOINTMENT, ACCEPTANCE & SCOPE. (a) Exclusive Appointment. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Distributor Agreement, Company hereby appoints and grants Distributor the exclusive right to sell and distribute the Products to customers that Distributor introduce and confirmed by Company, (in all territory of United State of America (USA) and to render other services as a distributor for Company as set forth herein. Distributor shall limit its activities with respect to the Products to Customers located within the Territory and refrain from selling or otherwise transferring, directly or indirectly, the Products to any person outside the Territory, without the express written consent of Company. Company shall not sell or otherwise supply, directly or indirectly, the Products to any Distributor's Customer's, except by sale through the Distributor. The Company shall not contact any of Distributor's Customer's for any reason, without the prior written approval of Distributor. Source: GENTECH HOLDINGS, INC., 1-A, 8/8/2019
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text by changing the nature of the distributorship from 'exclusive' to 'non-exclusive'. The original agreement grants the distributor the 'exclusive right to sell and distribute the Products', while the changed text removes this exclusivity. This creates a clear and impactful contradiction regarding the distributor's rights and the company's obligations, impacting the enforceability of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Distributor will promptly inspect the Products upon receipt at Distributor's facility to determine whether any Products included in the shipment are in short supply, defective, or otherwise not in conformance with this Agreement. Within Seven (7) days of receipt of such Products, Distributor will notify Company of any shortages, defects, non-conformance, and Company will promptly replace such Products free of charge. Title to the Products shall pass to Distributor upon delivery.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor will not be required to inspect the Products upon receipt at Distributor's facility. Any Products included in the shipment are deemed accepted as is. There is no timeframe for notification of any shortages, defects, non-conformance. Company is not obligated to replace such Products free of charge. Title to the Products shall pass to Distributor upon payment.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying Section 2(c) to remove the inspection obligation and change the title transfer introduces a contradiction with Section 3(c), which warrants the products will be free from defects. The changes now leave uncertainty on the Distributor's recourse if defective products are received. Changing when the title is passing to Distributor from 'upon delivery' to 'upon payment', can create contradiction in case of early defects.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) Company warrants and represents that the Products will be free from defects in design, materials and workmanship and conform with any specifications provided.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the Distributor's obligation to inspect products and states that they are deemed accepted as is, while also changing the title transfer to 'upon payment' instead of 'upon delivery'. This contradicts the original text which gave a seven day inspection window for defects and Section 3(c) which warrants the products to be free from defects. This discrepancy introduces a significant contradiction as the distributor has no recourse for defective products under the new terms, even though the company warrants them to be free from defects. The 'as is' clause directly conflicts with the warranty.

perturbation.original_text: Either Party may terminate this Agreement prior to its expiration upon the occurrence of either of the following: (i) the other Party becomes insolvent, or institutes (or there is instituted against it) proceedings in bankruptcy, insolvency, reorganization or dissolution, makes an assignment for the benefit of creditors or becomes nationalized or has any of its material assets confiscated or expropriated; or (ii) the other Party (in this case, the "breaching Party") fails to perform any of its obligations hereunder and fails to correct such failure within Ninety (90) calendar days after receiving written demand therefore from the non-breaching Party, specifying the failure in sufficient detail for the breaching Party to correct such failure; provided, however, that upon a second breach of the same obligation by such Party, the other Party may forthwith terminate this Agreement upon notice to the breaching Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Either Party may terminate this Agreement prior to its expiration for any reason, without notice or cause. The termination will be effective immediately upon delivery of notice.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies conditions under which the agreement can be terminated, including insolvency or failure to perform obligations with a 90-day correction period. The modification allows termination for any reason without notice, directly contradicting the earlier stipulations and creating uncertainty in situations where those specific breach conditions arise. This change creates an in-text contradiction with the original grounds for termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) Termination. Either Party may terminate this Agreement prior to its expiration upon the occurrence of either of the following: (i) the other Party becomes insolvent, or institutes (or there is instituted against it) proceedings in bankruptcy, insolvency, reorganization or dissolution, makes an assignment for the benefit of creditors or becomes nationalized or has any of its material assets confiscated or expropriated; or (ii) the other Party (in this case, the "breaching Party") fails to perform any of its obligations hereunder and fails to correct such failure within Ninety (90) calendar days after receiving written demand therefore from the non-breaching Party, specifying the failure in sufficient detail for the breaching Party to correct such failure; provided, however, that upon a second breach of the same obligation by such Party, the other Party may forthwith terminate this Agreement upon notice to the breaching Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows termination for any reason without notice, which directly contradicts the original text that specifies conditions for termination, including a 90-day correction period for breaches. The contradicted text outlines specific instances where termination is permissible. This conflict creates a strong in-text contradiction regarding the circumstances under which the agreement can be terminated.
